title: Canada caregiver amount: For spouse or common-law partner, or eligible dependant age 18 or older - Canada.ca
content:
## Canada caregiver amount
For spouse or common-law partner, or eligible dependant age 18 or older
- Tax year: 2025 | Prior tax years: 2024 and earlier

## Who can claim this amount
You may be able to claim this amount if all of the following apply:
- You supported your spouse or common-law partner (see line 30300 ) or another eligible dependant who is 18 years of age or older (see line 30400 )
- That person had a physical or mental infirmity
- Their net income for 2025 was between $8,624 and $28,798

## Before you claim this amount
Complete line 30300 (for spouse or common-law partner) or line 30400 (for eligible dependent) using Schedule 5 , Amounts for Spouse or Common-Law Partner and Dependants .
Include the base amount of $2,687 for either the spouse or common-law partner amount or the eligible dependant amount that is required, whichever applies to you.

## Supporting medical documents
You may need a signed statement from a medical practitioner that shows:
- when the infirmity began
- how long the infirmity is expected to last
You do not need a new statement if the CRA already has an approved Form T2201 , Disability Tax Credit Certificate , for the applicable period.

## How to claim this amount
- Get the net income for your spouse or common-law partner, or eligible dependant, from line 23600 of their return (or estimate the amount if they are not filing a return)
- Using Schedule 5 : Complete line 30300 or line 30400 , whichever applies Then complete the calculation for line 30425 using the base amount from line 30300 or line 30400
- Complete line 30300 or line 30400 , whichever applies
- Then complete the calculation for line 30425 using the base amount from line 30300 or line 30400
- Enter the result on line 30425 of your return
If you have a spouse or common-law partner and cannot claim this amount for an eligible dependant age 18 or older , you may be able to claim the Canada caregiver amount for other infirm dependants age 18 or older on line 30450 .
Only one person can claim this amount. You cannot split or share this amount with another person.

## Forms and publications
- Guide RC4064, Disability-Related Information
- Form T2201, Disability Tax Credit Certificate
- Persons with disabilities, their caregivers, and the CRA

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
